spec_match.rationale: The page promises a 'single-producer / single-consumer ring buffer in SharedArrayBuffer' but the script never instantiates one — simSpsc is a single-threaded main-thread simulator that increments a counter on a wall-clock schedule. The SAB / worker / lock-free queue described in the prose simply isn't there.

interactive.rationale: Duration input + run button + four-cell readout per column. But the underlying benchmark is a simulation, not an actual SharedArrayBuffer queue, so the interactivity is theatre.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb sells 'two workers swap messages as fast as they can' and 'consumer's spin' — neither happens. The page text and code disagree.

edge_cases.rationale: Because the implementation is a sim, none of the realistic SPSC edge cases (full queue, slow producer, slow consumer, cacheline bouncing) are exercised.

references.rationale: see-also lists the TC39 proposal but NOT https://chromestatus.com/feature/5106098833719296 — violates CLAUDE.md invariant 3.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout (including --accent-amber which is defined in the design system), no raw hex, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Build a real SPSC ring buffer in SharedArrayBuffer
openQuestions.detail: The page sells two workers + SharedArrayBuffer + ring buffer but never builds them. Spawn producer + consumer workers sharing a SAB-backed Int32Array ring (head, tail, slots[N]), the consumer spinning on slot-empty. Toggle Atomics.pause in the consumer's spin and report real messages-per-second / empty-probe-per-message ratios. Without this, the concept is a falsehood.

openQuestions.title: Add a non-isolated graceful degrade
openQuestions.detail: Once the real implementation lands, the no-isolation fallback should still illustrate the pattern — perhaps a single-worker simulation that admits in the UI that it can't measure the real cross-thread cost. Currently the page hides that the 'illustrative' numbers are entirely synthetic.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: see-also is missing https://chromestatus.com/feature/5106098833719296 — CLAUDE.md invariant 3 requires it on every concept page.

summary: The strongest narrative of the four atomics-pause concepts on paper, but the implementation is a sham — a main-thread simulator that has nothing to do with SharedArrayBuffer or workers. Rebuild it as a real SAB-backed ring with producer/consumer workers, or downgrade the prose to match what's actually running.
